titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Davenport Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 20, 2019 at 04:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection documents, primarily through correspondence, the Evita and Levi B. Davenport family. It includes 420 autograph and typescript letters, signed, addressed to members of the Davenport family, 1907-1964, undated. Many of the letters are between Evita Davenport and her husband, Levi B. Davenport, her mother, Eva M. Rait, and her son, Lee Davenport, Jr. Matters discussed primarily focus on Evita Davenport's family and social life in Minneapolis, Minnesota; Goldfield, Nevada; and San Francisco, California. Multiple letters are accompanied by fabric swatches and dried flowers. Later letters are from Lee Davenport, Jr. to his parents and document his military service. There are also letters written from Lucile Frank Reuben to Lee Davenport, Jr. during this time. Included in the collection are family papers, pertaining to both Levi B. Davenport and Lee Davenport, Jr., comprised of invoices, receipts, and certificates, 1923-1965, undated. There are also 41 photographs of people and places (mostly unidentified), circa 1860-1955, undated. Other papers include newspaper clippings; auditor's notices signed by J. H. Cogswell of Ipswich, Massachusetts; a land survey; a bank statement; food labels; and miscellaneous materials; 1842-1957, undated.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Tschanz Rare Books, LLC on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1907-1964, undated. II. Papers, 1842-1957, undated. III. Photographs, circa 1860-1955, undated.

bioghist.head: Davenport Family
bioghist.p: Evita Rait Davenport (1875-1962), daughter of James and Eva Rait, was married to Levi B. Davenport (1876-1964), a supervisor for various mining and gold refining companies in Goldfield, Nevada. Though living primarily in Minneapolis, Minnesota, she made multiple trips to Goldfield between 1908 and 1915, during which time the Davenports had 2 children: Donald Davenport (1910-1974) and Lee Davenport, Jr. (1915-1998). The Davenport family moved to San Francisco, California, by 1926. In early 1943, Lee Davenport, Jr., enlisted in the United States Army; by 1944 November, he was sent overseas to Europe and the Philippines. Lee Davenport, Jr., worked for the Veterans' Administration Guidance office in San Bernardino in 1947 and for the Travelers' Aid Society by 1952. He met Lucile Frank Reuben (1915-1998) in 1952, marrying her in 1954.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Davenport Family Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection documents, primarily through correspondence, the Evita and Levi B. Davenport family. Present are 420 autograph and typescript letters, signed, addressed to members of the Davenport family, 1907-1964, undated. Many of the letters are between Evita Davenport and her husband, Levi B. Davenport, her mother, Eva M. Rait, and her son, Lee Davenport, Jr. Matters discussed primarily focus on Evita Davenport's family and social life in Minneapolis, Minnesota; Goldfield, Nevada; and San Francisco, California. Multiple letters are accompanied by fabric swatches and dried flowers. Later letters are from Lee Davenport, Jr. to his parents and document his military service. There are also letters written from Lucile Frank Reuben to Lee Davenport, Jr. during this time. Included in the collection are family papers, pertaining to both Levi B. Davenport and Lee Davenport, Jr., comprised of invoices, receipts, and certificates, 1923-1965, undated. There are also 41 photographs of people and places (mostly unidentified), including Evita Davenport and Donald Davenport, circa 1860-1955, undated. Other papers include newspaper clippings pertaining to the Minneapolis public library; auditor's notices signed by J. H. Cogswell of Ipswich, Massachusetts; a land survey; a bank statement; food labels; and miscellaneous materials; 1842-1957, undated.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Davenport Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

geogname.part: California -- Social life and customs -- 20th Century
Minnesota -- Social life and customs -- 20th Century
Nevada -- Social life and customs -- 20th Century

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains correspondence of the Davenport family. Many of the letters are between Evita Davenport and her husband, Levi B. Davenport, her mother, Eva M. Rait, and her son, Lee Davenport, Jr. Matters discussed primarily focus on Evita Davenport's family and social life in Minneapolis, Minnesota; Goldfield, Nevada; and San Francisco, California. Multiple letters are accompanied by fabric swatches and dried flowers. Later letters are from Lee Davenport, Jr. to his parents and document his military service. There are also letters written from Lucile Frank Reuben to Lee Davenport, Jr. during this time.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains the papers of Levi B. Davenport and Lee Davenport, Jr., primarily receipts and invoices. It also includes Lee Davenport's National Red Cross first aid training certificate, elementary school certificate, and report card. A list of property belonging to Evita Rait Davenport and visiting cards of Davenport family members are included. Present are auditor's notices signed by J. H. Cogswell, a bank statement, 3 food labels, a land survey, and newspaper clippings pertaining to the Minneapolis public library and the funeral of James Addicott. Miscellaneous materials include an embroidered patch; Christmas festival ticket; blank check of the Italian-American Bank in San Francisco, California; and reproduction image of Earl Derby, Lord Carins, Sir S. Northcoat, and Gathorne Hardy.

did.unittitle: Certificates, report card, and wheat saving card
Life insurance receipts of Lee B. Davenport, Jr.
List of property belonging to Evita Rait Davenport

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains 41 photographs. Included are 6 photographs of Donald Davenport, 1 photograph of Evita Rait Davenport, 20 photographs of unidentified individuals, and 9 photographs of unidentified locations. 5 photographs have identifying information on the verso.
